projectDescription: An end-to-end data analysis project that applies ETL and Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) techniques to identify customer churn patterns in a fictional Latin American telecom company, delivering actionable insights to reduce attrition.
keyFeatures: ETL pipeline: extraction from nested JSON, flattening, and type conversion
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) with Pandas, Matplotlib, and Seaborn
Churn distribution analysis: 26.5% attrition rate identified
Segmentation by contract type, tenure, and subscription services
Feature engineering: daily spend metrics and service count quantification
Correlation analysis between demographic, service, and billing variables
Final report with strategic recommendations to reduce churn
publicApiSurface: cargar_datos_locales(ruta) — loads and validates JSON dataset into a DataFrame
Churn label column — binary target variable (Yes/No)
customer nested object — gender, SeniorCitizen, Partner, Dependents, tenure
internet nested object — InternetService, OnlineSecurity, OnlineBackup, DeviceProtection, TechSupport, StreamingTV, StreamingMovies
account nested object — Contract, PaperlessBilling, PaymentMethod, Charges.Monthly, Charges.Total
